cases.question: Which active accounts have the highest balances? Show the account number and current balance for the top 20.

CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account {status: "active"})
RETURN a.accountNumber AS accountNumber, a.balance AS balance
ORDER BY a.balance DESC

RETURN t.transactionID, t.amount, t.type, t.status, t.date,

cases.question: What does transaction activity look like across 2025? Break down the count and total value by transaction type.
cases.generated_cypher:

WHERE t.date >= date('2025-01-01') AND t.date <= date('2025-12-31')
RETURN t.type AS transactionType,
       count(t) AS transactionCount,
       round(sum(t.amount), 2) AS totalValue

cases.question: What is the average, highest, and total number of transactions for each transaction status (pending, completed, etc.)?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)
RETURN
  t.status                  AS status,
  count(t)                  AS totalTransactions,
  round(avg(t.amount), 2)   AS avgAmount,
  max(t.amount)             AS highestAmount
ORDER BY totalTransactions DESC

cases.question: Which accounts are indirectly linked to each other through chains of shared personal details? I want to see account pairs that are connected through two to four intermediate links.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{2,4}-(b:Account)
WHERE elementId(a) < elementId(b)
RETURN a.accountID AS accountA,
       a.accountType AS typeA,
       a.status AS statusA,
       b.accountID AS accountB,
       b.accountType AS typeB,
       b.status AS statusB,
       a.louvainCommunity AS communityA,
       b.louvainCommunity AS communityB
ORDER BY a.accountID
LIMIT 100

cases.question: What is the shortest chain of linked accounts connecting a savings account to a checking account?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH p = SHORTEST 1
  (start:Account {accountType: 'savings'})-[:TRANSACTED_TO|SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{1,}(end:Account {accountType: 'checking'})
RETURN
  [n IN nodes(p) | n.accountID]    AS accountChain,
  [r IN relationships(p) | type(r)] AS linkTypes,
  length(p)                          AS chainLength

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which customers sit at the center of the most financial connections in our network? For each, show whether they have any open alerts or investigations.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c:Customer)
WITH c ORDER BY c.betweenness DESC LIMIT 20
OPTIONAL MATCH (c)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a:Account)-[:HAS_ALERT]->(al:Alert)-[:HAS_STATUS]->(alertStatus:AlertStatus)
WHERE alertStatus.status IN ['open', 'investigating']
RETURN c.customerID           AS customerId,
       c.name                 AS customerName,
       c.betweenness          AS betweenness,
       c.pagerank             AS pagerank,
       c.fraudFlag            AS fraudFlag,
       count(DISTINCT al)     AS activeAlertCount,
       collect(DISTINCT {
         alertId:     al.alertId,
         description: al.description,
         severity:    al.severity,
         status:      alertStatus.status
       })                     AS activeAlerts
ORDER BY betweenness DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

WITH a, count(t) AS txCount
WHERE txCount > 3
RETURN a.accountNumber AS accountNumber, a.accountType AS accountType, txCount

cases.question: Which transactions are unusually large compared to other transactions of the same type? Show transactions that exceed the average for their category.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)
WITH t.type AS txType, avg(t.amount) AS avgAmount
WITH collect({type: txType, avgAmount: avgAmount}) AS typeAvgs
MATCH (tx:Transaction)
WITH tx, [ta IN typeAvgs WHERE ta.type = tx.type | ta.avgAmount][0] AS typeAvg
WHERE tx.amount > typeAvg
RETURN
  tx.transactionID          AS transactionID,
  tx.type                   AS type,
  tx.amount                 AS amount,
  tx.date                   AS date,
  tx.status                 AS status,
  round(typeAvg, 2)         AS typeAverage,
  round(tx.amount - typeAvg, 2)                        AS excessAmount,
  round((tx.amount - typeAvg) / typeAvg * 100.0, 1)   AS excessPct
ORDER BY excessPct DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Find pairs of accounts that share personal details where at least one account belongs to a customer flagged for fraud. Who owns these accounts?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a1:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-(a2:Account)
WHERE elementId(a1) < elementId(a2)
MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a1)
MATCH (c2:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a2)
WHERE c1.fraudFlag = true OR c2.fraudFlag = true
RETURN
  c1.name          AS owner1,
  c1.customerID    AS owner1ID,
  c1.fraudFlag     AS owner1Flagged,
  a1.accountID     AS account1ID,
  a1.accountType   AS account1Type,
  a1.status        AS account1Status,
  c2.name          AS owner2,
  c2.customerID    AS owner2ID,
  c2.fraudFlag     AS owner2Flagged,
  a2.accountID     AS account2ID,
  a2.accountType   AS account2Type,
  a2.status        AS account2Status
ORDER BY c1.fraudFlag DESC, c2.fraudFlag DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which accounts received unusually large sums of money within a single month in 2025? Flag any account that received more than $10,000 in one calendar month.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)-[:RECEIVED]->(a:Account)
WHERE t.date.year = 2025
  AND t.status = 'completed'
WITH a,
     t.date.year AS yr,
     t.date.month AS mo,
     sum(t.amount) AS monthlyTotal
WHERE monthlyTotal > 10000
RETURN a.accountID       AS accountID,
       a.accountNumber   AS accountNumber,
       a.accountType     AS accountType,
       a.status          AS accountStatus,
       yr                AS year,
       mo                AS month,
       round(monthlyTotal, 2) AS totalReceived
ORDER BY totalReceived DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Are there customers in the same fraud network cluster who are also sharing phone numbers or email addresses with each other? This could indicate coordinated activity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:USES_EMAIL|USES_PHONE]->(shared)<-[:USES_EMAIL|USES_PHONE]-(c2:Customer)
WHERE c1.louvainCommunity = c2.louvainCommunity
  AND c1.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
  AND c1.customerID < c2.customerID
WITH c1, c2,
     collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN shared:Email        THEN shared.email       END) AS sharedEmails,
     collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN shared:PhoneNumber  THEN shared.phoneNumber END) AS sharedPhones
RETURN c1.louvainCommunity                        AS fraudCluster,
       c1.name                                    AS customer1,
       c1.fraudFlag                               AS customer1Flagged,
       c2.name                                    AS customer2,
       c2.fraudFlag                               AS customer2Flagged,
       sharedEmails,
       sharedPhones,
       size(sharedEmails) + size(sharedPhones)    AS totalSharedIdentifiers
ORDER BY fraudCluster ASC, totalSharedIdentifiers DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

RETURN sender.accountNumber AS senderAccount,

       t.amount             AS amount,

       t.status             AS status,
       receiver.accountNumber AS receiverAccount,

ORDER BY t.amount DESC

cases.question: Among the most influential accounts in our network, which pairs are most directly connected through shared identity links? Show the tightest connections first.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH p = SHORTEST 1 (a:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{1,4}(b:Account)
WHERE elementId(a) < elementId(b)
  AND a.pagerank IS NOT NULL
  AND b.pagerank IS NOT NULL
WITH a, b, length(p) AS hopCount,
     a.pagerank + b.pagerank AS combinedInfluence
ORDER BY hopCount ASC, combinedInfluence DESC
RETURN
  a.accountID             AS accountA,
  round(a.pagerank, 4)    AS pagerankA,
  b.accountID             AS accountB,
  round(b.pagerank, 4)    AS pagerankB,
  hopCount,
  round(combinedInfluence, 4) AS combinedInfluence
LIMIT 20

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Are there active accounts sending money through multiple intermediate accounts before reaching a destination — a pattern that could indicate layering in money laundering? Show account pairs with multiple such routing paths.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH p = (src:Account {status: 'active'})-[:TRANSACTED_TO]->{3,5}(dst:Account {status: 'active'})
WHERE src <> dst
  AND ALL(n IN nodes(p) WHERE n.status = 'active')
WITH src, dst,
     count(p)                             AS routingPathCount,
     collect(DISTINCT size(nodes(p)) - 1) AS distinctHopCounts
WHERE routingPathCount >= 2
RETURN
  src.accountID        AS sourceAccountID,
  src.accountNumber    AS sourceAccountNumber,
  dst.accountID        AS destinationAccountID,
  dst.accountNumber    AS destinationAccountNumber,
  routingPathCount,
  distinctHopCounts
ORDER BY routingPathCount DESC
LIMIT 20

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Search for customers with the surname 'Poole' and trace how many accounts in our system are linked to their accounts — directly or through up to three degrees of shared identity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('customerNames', 'Poole') YIELD node AS customer, score
WITH customer
MATCH (customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(startAccount:Account)
OPTIONAL MATCH (startAccount)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{1,3}(linkedAccount:Account)
WHERE linkedAccount <> startAccount
RETURN customer.name        AS customerName,
       customer.customerID  AS customerID,
       collect(DISTINCT startAccount.accountID) AS ownedAccountIDs,
       count(DISTINCT linkedAccount)             AS linkedAccountCount
ORDER BY linkedAccountCount DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected
